title: Podman pull gets wrong architecture image regardless of setting --arch
body:
### Issue Description

I use this command to get an image:
```
podman pull ghcr.io/gem5/ubuntu-24.04_all-dependencies:latest
```
It starts pulling `arm64` image but my system is `amd64`. I also use `--arch=amd64` but it still tries to get the arm architecture image.

Though the image has published with both architectures (can be seen [here](https://github.com/gem5/gem5/pkgs/container/ubuntu-24.04_all-dependencies)).

Only using the exact hash of the image can force podman to get the right image:
```
podman pull ghcr.io/gem5/ubuntu-24.04_all-dependencies:latest@sha256:6508f1894d01c13c8345e5c4894c27e1cd0442977663bc52d6462d60163c3517
```
It doesn't work with setting the flags.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. run command `podman pull --arch=amd64 ghcr.io/gem5/ubuntu-24.04_all-dependencies:latest` on a amd64 system



### Describe the results you received

arm64 image is pulled. 

### Describe the results you expected

It should pull the specified architecture.

comments.body: Oh my bad, it was my fault.
